name: Villarrobledo and Socuéllamos — return through the fields of La Mancha
name_original: Villarrobledo y Socuéllamos — vuelta por los campos de La Mancha
region_intro: Mountain biking around Villarrobledo offers a diverse landscape, primarily characterized by extensive plains and the world's largest area of vineyards. Situated at elevations between 600 and 800 meters above sea level, the region features routes that traverse these unique agricultural landscapes. Specific natural features include the pine forests of Pinar de Vadohondo and the areas around the Záncara River and its tributaries, providing varied terrain for cyclists. The area also includes gentle ascents to points like the Ermita de San Cristóbal, offering panoramic views. Best mountain bike trails around Villarrobledo The most popular mountain bike trail is Villarrobledo and Socuéllamos — return through the fields of La Mancha, a 32.7 miles (52.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 1 minute to complete. This route offers scenic riding through the vast vineyard-laden plains of La Mancha. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Truncated Lives Monument – Pinar de Vadohondo loop from Villarrobledo, a moderate 31.4 miles (50.5 km) path. This route explores the natural pine forests and open countryside surrounding Villarrobledo. Local mountain bikers also love the Villarrobledo – Hermitage of San Cristóbal loop from Villarrobledo, a 36.9 miles (59.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain including a climb to the Hermitage of San Cristóbal, often completed in about 5 hours 1 minute. Mountain biking around Villarrobledo is defined by vineyard-laden plains, pine forests, and river systems. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides across flat terrain to more challenging routes with moderate elevation changes. The routes in Villarrobledo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villarrobledo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Villarrobledo?
A: There are nearly 50 mountain bike trails around Villarrobledo, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 200 times, with an average rating of 4.8 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Villarrobledo?
A: The terrain around Villarrobledo is primarily characterized by extensive plains and the world's largest area of vineyards, situated at elevations between 600 and 800 meters above sea level. You'll find scenic rides through these agricultural landscapes, as well as varied terrain including pine forests in areas like Pinar de Vadohondo and gentle ascents to points such as the Ermita de San Cristóbal.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Villarrobledo?
A: Yes, while most routes are moderate, there are a couple of easy mountain bike trails available. These typically offer less technical riding across the flatter vineyard plains, perfect for those new to mountain biking or looking for a relaxed ride.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes starting from Villarrobledo?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes around Villarrobledo are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Truncated Lives Monument – Pinar de Vadohondo loop from Villarrobledo&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option exploring pine forests and open countryside.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: You can explore various natural features and landmarks. Routes often pass through the vast vineyard-laden plains, pine forests like the Pinar de Vadohondo, and areas near the Záncara River. You might also encounter cultural landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5934218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Blas Church&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5934206" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de los Tellez&lt;/a&gt; within Villarrobledo itself.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that offer significant climbs or more challenging sections?
A: Yes, for those seeking more challenging rides, there are routes with moderate elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31865133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villarrobledo – Hermitage of San Cristóbal loop from Villarrobledo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult trail that includes a climb to the Hermitage of San Cristóbal, offering varied terrain and rewarding views.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Villarrobledo?
A: The mountain bike trails in Villarrobledo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the unique experience of cycling through the extensive vineyard landscapes, the peaceful pine forests, and the variety of routes suitable for different abilities.

Q: Are there any family-friendly cycling routes in the Villarrobledo area?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' mountain bike routes are not explicitly categorized, the region's generally flat plains and easy trails offer suitable options for families. Look for routes marked as 'easy' or those that stick to the less technical vineyard paths for a pleasant family outing.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that pass through or near the Pinar de Vadohondo?
A: Yes, the Pinar de Vadohondo is a notable natural feature in the area, offering pine forests and varied terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38172684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinar de Vadohondo – Ermita de San Cristóbal loop from Villarrobledo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail that specifically incorporates this area into its route.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail available around Villarrobledo?
A: One of the longer routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31865133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villarrobledo – Hermitage of San Cristóbal loop from Villarrobledo&lt;/a&gt;, which spans nearly 60 kilometers (59.3 km) and typically takes around 5 hours to complete. This trail offers a substantial ride through varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any trails that explore the famous vineyard landscapes of La Mancha?
A: Absolutely. Villarrobledo is known for its vast vineyards, and many trails traverse these iconic landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040859593" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villarrobledo and Socuéllamos — return through the fields of La Mancha&lt;/a&gt; route is an excellent example, offering scenic riding directly through the vineyard-laden plains.
tips: The path is narrow and has some very fun jumps. In addition, this pine forest is ideal in summer, since there are many leftovers.
The bridge is located over the Córcoles river next to an old hydraulic mill, it allows an accessible passage of the river when it carries water.

It is located in the upper part of the city, on the Cerro de San Cristóbal. It was built in the 18th century and is dedicated to San Cristóbal, patron saint of drivers and travelers. From the hill you can see the vineyard fields that surround the city.

waypoints.name: Mill Bridge over the Córcoles River
